user_message: I have this email thread from Lucerne Publishing that needs identify the blockers. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 14:51 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with whitespace analysis. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 08:25 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 13:21 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-G trap (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-G failure mode (Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-G -- Confuses individual contact return with account re-emergence
